commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an OpenRouter music-generation provider, registers it in the OpenRouter plugin and manifest, updates OpenRouter/music-generation docs, and expands the bundled music-provider contract expectation.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible for the feature gap. Current main has no OpenRouter music provider registration or manifest contract, so music_generate action=list cannot expose OpenRouter music without this provider path.
commands.review_followup: A narrow docs consistency repair can be automated if a maintainer promotes it; the provider implementation otherwise needs normal PR CI/review.

target.title: feat: add OpenRouter music generation provider
target.body:
## Summary

Adds OpenRouter music generation provider support. OpenRouter supports audio/music models (e.g., Google Lyria) but the provider was never wired up in OpenClaw's generation catalog.

## Background

- PR #64513 (April 2026) originally covered image + video + music but was closed without merging
- Image and video providers were later shipped separately
- Music generation was never picked back up — `music_generate action=list` shows no OpenRouter entry

## What this does

Wires the OpenRouter music generation provider so `music_generate` works with OpenRouter models (e.g., `google/lyria-3-pro-preview`, `google/lyria-3-clip-preview`).

Follows the same patterns as the existing video generation provider (SSRF-guarded via `fetchWithTimeoutGuarded`, private-network policy threaded through).

## Testing

- Branch rebased against `origin/main`
- Provider registers correctly in tool catalog
- Generation pipeline functional

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: The `music_generate` tool with provider=openrouter and model=google/lyria-3-clip-preview produces valid MP3 audio output through OpenRouter's chat completions streaming audio API. The provider registers correctly in the tool catalog alongside other bundled music providers.

- **Real environment tested**: copperfox (Raspberry Pi, arm64, Node v22.22.0) — OpenClaw dev instance with debug-level tracing enabled, OpenRouter API with google/lyria-3-clip-preview model, branch feat/openrouter-music-generation rebased on origin/main.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Start OpenClaw dev instance with the music generation provider registered
  2. Invoke `music_generate` tool with prompt "a short comedy track about an AI and its tools" via OpenRouter → google/lyria-3-clip-preview
  3. Run a second generation with debug-level tracing enabled to capture full end-to-end logs

- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from two independent trace-logged sessions on copperfox:

  **Generation 1:**
  ```
  Track UUID: 52a7b61c-363b-4d12-8135-442e12d9dddf
  File: 732,073 bytes, MPEG ADTS layer III v1, 192 kbps, 44.1 kHz, JntStereo
  ```

  **Generation 2 (debug trace, 2026-05-11 00:59 BST):**
  ```
  embedded run tool start: tool=music_generate toolCallId=914fabeb7
  music-generate-tool: 3ms (alongside image: 3ms, video: 6ms)
  embedded run start:  runId=music_generate:202b5607-9bd3-4aea-9e98-2b57f89e4323:ok
                      provider=openrouter model=openrouter/auto
  embedded run end:    isError=false durationMs=1318
  MEDIA:/home/dixie/.openclaw/media/tool-music-generation/track-1---6c581871-70b4-4247-9d70-2e3bc4b791f5.mp3

  Track UUID: 6c581871-70b4-4247-9d70-2e3bc4b791f5
  File: 744,611 bytes, MPEG ADTS layer III v1, 192 kbps, 44.1 kHz, JntStereo
  ```

  Contract test `checks-node-core-fast` passed after adding `openrouter` to `EXPECTED_BUNDLED_MUSIC_PROVIDER_PLUGIN_IDS`. SSRF guard pattern matches video provider (`fetchWithTimeoutGuarded` with `auditContext: "openrouter-music"`).

- **Observed result after fix**: Two independent music generation runs both produced valid MP3 output with `isError=false`. The provider registered correctly in the tool catalog alongside other bundled providers. The contract test passed with the updated expected provider list.

- **What was not tested**: WAV format output (OpenRouter only returns MP3 regardless of requested format); google/lyria-3-pro-preview model variation (tested with clip-preview; pro-preview uses same API codepath); generation with reference image input (explicitly unsupported — throws error); edit/inpaint music operations; long-duration tracks beyond 30 seconds.
target.author: dixiefixie

commands.reason: repair commands require an open issue or PR
